# 24Houring

> 24Houring (https://24houring.com) is a free, no-signup web app that visualizes a
> day as a 24-hour circular timetable (a clock-style pie chart). Users plan, edit,
> save and share their daily schedule. It runs fully client-side — data is stored
> only on the user's device, it works offline, and it supports 7 languages. There
> is no account, no paywall, and no installation required.

## What it is

- A circular **24-hour day planner / timetable** that shows the whole day at a glance.
- Also offers **12-hour clock views**: Day (06:00–18:00) and Night (18:00–06:00). All views edit the same underlying day, so a change in one view appears in the others.
- Edit by dragging time-block boundaries; split a block with one click (scissors) and merge by removing a divider.

## Key features

- Lifestyle **presets** (student, office worker, etc.) and **multi-day** schedules.
- **Export** to PNG, PDF and JSON; full **backup and restore**.
- **Share** the timetable as an image to Instagram, KakaoTalk and other apps (Web Share API).
- **Add to home screen** (installable PWA); **offline** capable; **7 languages** (Korean, English, Japanese, Chinese, French, Spanish, Russian).
- Free and private: no sign-up, data kept on-device.

## How to use

1. Pick a preset or start from an empty day.
2. Click the ring to split time blocks; name them and set a color/icon; drag boundaries to adjust times.
3. Export (PNG/PDF/JSON) or share the timetable as an image.

## FAQ

- **Is it free?** Yes — completely free, no sign-up.
- **Can I switch to a 12-hour clock view?** Yes — toggle 24h → Day (06–18) → Night (18–06); all views stay linked.
- **Can I save or share my timetable?** Yes — export to PNG/PDF/JSON or share the image directly.
- **What languages are supported?** Korean, English, Japanese, Chinese, French, Spanish, Russian.

## Links

- App: https://24houring.com/
- Sitemap: https://24houring.com/sitemap.xml
